title: Permutahedral Subdivisions and Class Formulas from Coxeter Elements
speaker: Melissa Sherman-Bennett
abstract: I will discuss some regular subdivisions of the permutahedron, one for each Coxeter element in the symmetric group. These subdivisions are "Bruhat interval" subdivisions, meaning that each face is the convex hull of the permutations in a Bruhat interval (regarded as vectors). Bruhat interval subdivisions in general correspond to cones in the positive tropical flag variety by work of Joswig-Loho-Luber-Olarte and Boretsky-Eur-Williams; the subdivisions indexed by Coxeter elements are finest subdivisions and so correspond to a subset of the maximal cones. Relatedly, we also obtain new formulas for the class of the permutahedral variety as a sum of Richardson classes in the cohomology ring of the flag variety. This is joint work with Allen Knutson and Mario Sanchez.
tags: Workshop on Combinatorics of Enumerative Geometry

proof_stages.label: Flag-variety subvarieties
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up the type-A flag variety G/B with B, B-minus and torus actions, introduces the permutahedral variety as a generic torus-orbit closure, and defines closed Richardson varieties from opposite Bruhat cells.

proof_stages.label: Moment-map dictionary to Bruhat interval polytopes
proof_stages.summary: Taking moment-map images turns Richardson varieties into Bruhat interval polytopes P_{v,w}, with the full permutahedron appearing as P_{e,w0}, so the geometric objects acquire a polyhedral combinatorial shadow.

proof_stages.label: Coxeter-indexed subdivision and class formula theorem
proof_stages.summary: After defining regular subdivisions and Coxeter elements, the main theorem states that for any Coxeter element c the polytopes P_{w,wc} with length-additive product form the maximal cells of a finest regular subdivision of the permutahedron, and the same indices give a degeneration of the permutahedral variety to Richardson varieties and hence a cohomology class formula.

proof_stages.label: Separation from toric Groebner degenerations and prior special cases
proof_stages.summary: The speaker explains that the subdivision statement and degeneration statement look morally related by toric degeneration philosophy but are presently proved separately, and positions the standard-Coxeter case against known cubical-subdivision and cohomology results.

proof_stages.label: Flag-positroid framework and combinatorial payoff
proof_stages.summary: Bruhat interval polytopes are identified as flag positroid polytopes, placing the theorem inside the positive flag Dressian or positive tropical flag variety framework and showing that it supplies an infinite family of maximal cones and subdivisions with varying numbers of maximal cells.

proof_stages.label: General H_{v,t} degeneration theorem
proof_stages.summary: The degeneration part is generalized from the permutahedral variety to varieties H_{v,t} defined by the condition that a flag conjugates a generic torus element into a specified Bruhat cell, recovering the permutahedral case when v is Coxeter and yielding analogous Richardson-union and class formulas.

proof_stages.label: Double-Schubert proof strategy
proof_stages.summary: The proof lifts to the double flag variety, realizes H_{v,t} as the projection of a transverse intersection of shifted diagonal and v-indexed double Schubert varieties, degenerates the double Schubert factors simultaneously to products of Schubert and opposite Schubert varieties, and then projects back so only the full-dimensional Richardson components remain.
